page: Microsoft Azure FX32-16mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading operating at a high base frequency of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with a massive 672.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 42.0 GB per core. It includes 1890 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are present on this instance type.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2315.0 and a Geekbench text processing score of 2959.0, both placing in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded operations perform well, as evidenced by a gzip compression speed of 34.78 GB/sec and a bzip3 decompression speed of 14.22 GB/sec, both in the top 25%. Large block memory write bandwidth is also strong at 91.91 GB/sec. Multi-core performance, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 13710.0 and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 17759.48, falls into the average category.

Given the high memory density of 42.0 GB per core and a high-frequency 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, this server offers a specialized hardware profile. The resource allocation favors memory-intensive workloads over massive parallel multi-core scaling, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets but moderate parallel compute. It is suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching, enterprise applications, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure FX32-16mds_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the FXmdsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores operating at 4.2 GHz, paired with 672.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage. The server exhibits strong single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 10% tier, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression and large-block memory write speeds. Multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng, align with average tier performance. Lacking a GPU, this configuration is tailored for memory-intensive, single-thread-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise application hosting, and data processing tasks that demand substantial memory capacity per core without requiring massive parallel CPU scaling.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-16mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 4.2 GHz processor, 672 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-16mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive server featuring exceptional memory-to-core density and top-tier single-core performance for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor
672.0 GB of system memory providing 42.0 GB per physical core
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Best suited for large in-memory databases and enterprise applications
